name: Bytesafe
license: Proprietary, closed source (Bitfront AB EULA; "all rights reserved", per the site footer and LICENSE.txt). Source code is not published; the GitHub repo holds only deployment manifests, docs, LICENSE.txt/EULA.md, and a cosign.pub. The Community Edition is free for both commercial and personal use; CE binaries and Docker images are Sigstore/cosign-signed.
cost: Billed in EUR. Free self-hosted Community Edition (on-premise, companies of any size). Hosted/cloud tiers: Professional €59/user/month; Business €1,100/month flat (100 users included); Enterprise custom annual pricing (100+ users, cloud or on-premise add-on). Public open-source projects can get a free hosted Community account on request; new signups auto-activate a free trial of a premium plan (no credit card). Also listed on AWS Marketplace. (Older third-party listings quote ~$1,100; the vendor prices and bills in EUR.)
language: Unknown (proprietary, closed source; the CE ships as a signed multi-arch binary plus a Docker image `bytesafe/bytesafe-ce`, source not published)
last_updated: 2024-09-15 (Community Edition v1.0.13, latest GitHub release); SaaS actively developed as of 2026 (Professional plan, SBOM Observer SCA add-on)
perf_notes: No public throughput/latency/memory benchmarks published. CE is a single application binary (default port 8081) fronting PostgreSQL (config, package metadata, vuln data, encrypted secrets) plus Redis (cache, no persistent data); package artifacts live on the local filesystem (default /var/lib/bytesafe). On-demand upstream fetches are cached so repeat installs are served locally.
scale_notes: Managed SaaS is positioned as highly available; HA/DR, audit trails, custom SAML, and data residency (EU default; US/APAC add-on) are reserved for Business/Enterprise tiers. Self-hosted Community Edition is effectively single-node (one app instance + Postgres + Redis + local blob volume) and capped at 10 registries/firewalls and 1 upstream per firewall. Docker images are multi-arch (amd64/arm64) and Kubernetes manifests are provided; no official Helm chart or documented multi-node clustering for CE.
summary: A multi-ecosystem "dependency firewall" and private package registry (npm/yarn/pnpm, PyPI, Maven/Gradle, NuGet) that proxies/caches upstreams, hosts private packages, and gates them with vulnerability, license, and policy controls, delivered as SaaS plus a free self-hosted Community Edition.
extras: Dependency Firewall with rich policy types: allow-only, block, block-downstream, block install-scripts, quarantine-new (review + attestation gate), delay-upstream, freeze (read-only), immutable-versions
Software Composition Analysis (SCA) via the SBOM Observer add-on with SBOM generation/export and license-compliance policy
Multi-ecosystem registries from one platform: npm/yarn/pnpm, Maven/Gradle, NuGet, and PyPI (Python via pip)
SAML SSO (Azure AD, Google, Okta, Auth0) plus social SSO (GitHub/Google/Microsoft) and 2FA on cloud/paid tiers; CE is limited to email+password and RBAC
Per-project registries and upstream chaining for grouped/virtual indexes
Integrations: GitHub, Slack, Zapier (Teams, Jira, and more), plus plugins/webhooks for workflow automation
CE binaries and Docker images are signed with Sigstore/cosign (cosign.pub published in repo) for verifiable provenance
Data-at-rest encryption of secrets via mandatory DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY env var; URL_PREFIX also required
CLI tool for registry/package/token management; anonymous opt-out telemetry (TELEMETRY=false)
Data residency options EU (default) / US / APAC on Enterprise; audit trails and custom SAML are Enterprise add-ons
Listed on AWS Marketplace for subscription via an AWS account
